name: Haena Beach Park Camping
highlights: Closest camping to the Kalalau trailhead
Five campsites on the beach at Hāʻena
Short walk from Ke'e Beach and Limahuli Garden
One of the most atmospheric camping locations in the state
seasonalNotes: Year-round. North shore surf is rough in winter — beach conditions change dramatically by season.

name: Kōkeʻe State Park Cabins
location: Kōkeʻe State Park — highlands above Waimea Canyon (3,600 ft)
highlights: Rustic cabins in the highlands
Cool temperatures at 3,600 feet
Access to Waimea Canyon and Alaka'i Swamp trails
Birdsong from species found nowhere else on Earth
Extremely affordable
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Cool and misty — bring layers. A completely different Kauaʻi from the coast.

name: Polihale State Park Camping
highlights: Remote camping on the westernmost beach on Kauaʻi
17 miles of empty beach backed by Nā Pali cliffs
Exceptional darkness for stargazing
The best sunsets on the island
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Rough dirt road in — 4WD or high clearance recommended in wet conditions.

name: Hanalei Colony Resort
highlights: Only true beachfront property in Hanalei
No TVs, no phones in rooms
Simple condos facing the beach
Best base for Kalalau Trail day hikers
bookingWindow: Book 3–6 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. North Shore location means bigger winter surf — summer is calmer for swimming.

name: Kauaʻi Inn
highlights: Most practical affordable hotel on the island
Well-located near the airport
Clean, simple, operated with care
Good first and last night option
bookingWindow: Book 1–2 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Practical, not scenic — a base, not a destination.

name: Na Pali Coast Cottages
highlights: Small independent cottage rentals on the north shore
Locally owned, long-established
The north shore cottage experience is a Kauaʻi original
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 months ahead — varies by property
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Quality and character vary — look for locally owned, long-established listings.

name: Waimea Plantation Cottages
location: Waimea (West Side) — coconut grove near black-sand beach
highlights: Restored plantation-era cottages from the early 1900s
Spread across a coconut grove
Steps from the black-sand beach at Waimea
Best base for Waimea Canyon and Kōkeʻe exploration
Sunsets over Niʻihau from the beach are unmatched
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. No resort amenities, no crowds — just history, stillness, and the garden isle as it was.

name: Aston at Poipū Kai
highlights: Condo-style resort in a 110-acre tropical setting
Full kitchens — practical for families and longer stays
Quieter and more affordable than beachfront resorts
Walking distance to south shore's best beaches and restaurants
bookingWindow: Book 2–3 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Budget-friendly south shore base with more space than a hotel room.

name: Grand Hyatt Kauaʻi Resort & Spa
location: Poipū (South Shore) — beachfront at Shipwreck Beach
highlights: The grandest property on the island
Lagoon pools, full Anara Spa with Hawaiian healing traditions
Beachfront access to Shipwreck Beach
The south shore's most complete luxury experience
bookingWindow: Reserve well ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Anara Spa's lomilomi and pohaku treatments are grounded in Hawaiian tradition.

name: Timbers Kauaʻi
highlights: Newer luxury property on a former plantation estate
Organic farm on-site
Direct ocean access, considered design
Less crowded than Poipū — a quieter base
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The organic farm is a genuine differentiator — not marketing.

name: 1 Hotel Hanalei Bay
location: Princeville (North Shore) — bluff above Hanalei Bay
highlights: Former St. Regis Princeville, comprehensively renovated
North Shore's defining luxury property
Perched on a bluff above Hanalei Bay with Nā Pali views
Sustainability-focused: reclaimed materials, farm-to-table, on-site garden
Spa rooted in Hawaiian and plant-based practices
Pū Poa Beach below — one of the best on the island
bookingWindow: Reserve well ahead — the only full-service luxury on the North Shore
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The renovation brought genuine sustainability ethos, not just branding.

name: Koloa Landing Resort
highlights: Best pool facilities on the island
Multiple lagoon-style pools, lazy river, water slides
Adults-only infinity pool
Villa-style units with full kitchens
Walking distance to Poipū Beach and Kukuiʻula Village
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Strong choice for families with children on the south shore.

name: Outrigger Kauaʻi Beach Resort & Spa
highlights: Beachfront on Poipū Beach — walk from room to water in under a minute
Recently renovated rooms
On-site dining and full spa
Reliable snorkeling and turtle sightings
bookingWindow: Book 2–3 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Less grand than Grand Hyatt, more accessible — solid middle-luxury option.
